title: Activity: Build a Data Viewer
description: Write fetch code from scratch to display data from a public API.

blocks.title: What to expect
blocks.content: In this activity you will write fetch code from scratch (or with minimal starter code) to retrieve and display data from a public API. Each task builds on the previous one.

blocks.title: Instructions
blocks.content:
Using the DummyJSON API, write a fetch function that retrieves user number 1 and prints their first name, last name, and email to the console.

Endpoint: https://dummyjson.com/users/1

blocks.title: Task 1

blocks.body: You need two await expressions — one for fetch() and one for response.json(). Then access data.firstName, data.lastName, and data.email.

blocks.title: Instructions
blocks.content:
Fetch the first 5 posts from the DummyJSON posts endpoint and print each post's title on a separate line.

Endpoint: https://dummyjson.com/posts?limit=5

blocks.title: Task 2

blocks.body: After parsing the JSON, use data.posts.forEach() to loop over the array and console.log(post.title) for each one.

blocks.title: Instructions
blocks.content: Copy your solution from Task 2 into the playground below and wrap it in a try/catch block. Then also add a check for response.ok — if the response is not OK, throw an error with the status code. Test that it works by temporarily using an invalid URL.

blocks.title: Task 3

blocks.body: After the first await, add: if (!response.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP error: ${response.status}`);

blocks.title: Extension task
blocks.content: Fetch 20 posts from dummyjson.com/posts?limit=20, then use .filter() to keep only posts with more than 1000 views. Print the title and view count of each filtered post.

blocks.title: Extension
